title: Feedback Alignment Optimizer as Tactus Procedure
description:
Package the core feedback evaluation optimization loop with RCA as a standalone, repeatable Tactus procedure that can be executed programmatically, integrated into software as an agentic feature, and run outside IDE environments.

## Context

The plexus-scorecard-improver skill defines a 7-phase workflow for alignment optimization, but Phase 6-7 (the iterative optimization loop) exists only as a skill description. This epic implements it as a production-ready Tactus procedure.

## Business Value

- **Repeatability**: Optimization runs are reproducible across sessions
- **Automation**: Can be integrated into CI/CD and automated workflows  
- **Persistence**: Checkpoint/resume capability for long-running optimizations
- **Programmatic Access**: Can be invoked from Python applications
- **Production Ready**: HITL gates, error handling, comprehensive logging

## Technical Approach

- Tactus-based procedure with Lua DSL orchestration
- File-based YAML definition stored in git
- Database runtime instances with checkpoint persistence
- MCP tool integration for Plexus operations
- Flexible identifier resolution (name/key/ID/externalID)

## Scope

**IN SCOPE:**
- Tactus procedure YAML definition
- Baseline feedback evaluation with RCA
- Iterative optimization loop with HITL gates
- Fixed baseline metric tracking
- CLI command: plexus procedure optimize
- Programmatic Python API
- Comprehensive documentation

**OUT OF SCOPE:**
- plexus score optimize CLI shortcut (separate epic)
- Multi-score parallel optimization
- Auto-revert on regression
- Dashboard visualization

## Definition of Done

- All stories pass their Gherkin specifications
- 3+ successful optimizations on real scores
- Measurable AC1 improvements demonstrated
- Checkpoint/resume tested and working
- Documentation complete with real examples
- Code reviewed and merged to main

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
## Epic Started

Created complete issue hierarchy with BDD specifications:

**Stories Created:**
- plx-b7420a: Run baseline feedback evaluation with RCA
- plx-f5ba02: Iterative optimization loop with HITL gates  
- plx-9c3e02: CLI command with flexible identifier resolution
- plx-960a54: Checkpoint and resume capability
- plx-8d9799: Programmatic Python API

All stories include complete Gherkin specifications following The Way.

## Current Implementation Status

**COMPLETED** (during planning phase):
1. ✅ Tactus procedure YAML definition: `/plexus/procedures/feedback_alignment_optimizer.yaml`
2. ✅ Phase A (Setup & Baseline) implemented in Lua DSL
3. ✅ Phase B (Optimization Loop with HITL) implemented in Lua DSL
4. ✅ Phase C (Finalization) implemented in Lua DSL
5. ✅ MCP evaluation comparison tool: `/MCP/tools/evaluation/evaluation_comparison.py`
6. ✅ Tool registered in `/MCP/server.py`
7. ✅ Python API wrapper: `/plexus/agentic/alignment_optimizer.py`
8. ✅ CLI command: `plexus procedure optimize` in `/plexus/cli/procedure/procedures.py`
9. ✅ Comprehensive documentation: `/plexus/procedures/README_ALIGNMENT_OPTIMIZER.md`
10. ✅ YAML syntax validated successfully

## Files Created/Modified

**New Files:**
- `plexus/procedures/feedback_alignment_optimizer.yaml` (644 lines)
- `MCP/tools/evaluation/evaluation_comparison.py` (110 lines)
- `plexus/agentic/__init__.py`
- `plexus/agentic/alignment_optimizer.py` (276 lines)
- `plexus/procedures/README_ALIGNMENT_OPTIMIZER.md` (450+ lines)

**Modified Files:**
- `MCP/server.py` (added evaluation_comparison import and registration)
- `plexus/cli/procedure/procedures.py` (added optimize command, ~150 lines)

## Next Steps

Now need to **test with real data** to verify production readiness:
1. Run smoke tests (YAML syntax, tool availability)
2. Dry run with real score
3. Single iteration with real updates
4. Full optimization cycle
5. Checkpoint/resume test
6. Programmatic API test
7. Edge case handling

Creating tasks for these test phases next...

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
## Optimization Test In Progress

### Updates Made

Added `max_samples` parameter to support limiting feedback samples per evaluation:

**Files Modified:**
1. `plexus/procedures/feedback_alignment_optimizer.yaml`
   - Added `max_samples` parameter (default: null = all samples)
   - Updated both evaluation calls to pass max_samples

2. `plexus/cli/procedure/procedures.py`
   - Added `--max-samples` CLI option
   - Pass to procedure params

### Test Running

Currently running full optimization test:
- **Target:** Scorecard 731, Score 47086  
- **Days:** 180
- **Max samples:** 100 per evaluation
- **Max iterations:** 3
- **Command:** plexus procedure optimize -s "AW Confirmation" -c "Transfer Call?" --days 180 --max-samples 100 --max-iterations 3

Process running in background. Will update with results when complete.
